source.title: BSEE Accident Investigation Report - Structural Damage Crane Man Rider Incident

context.region: Gulf of America - Green Canyon Block 432

event.top_event: Uncontrolled descent of man rider during personnel lift operation
event.incident_type: Personnel handling incident - man rider malfunction
actions_taken: Personnel medivaced to onshore medical facility for evaluation
Joint investigation conducted with winch manufacturer technicians
BSEE onsite investigation performed on February 24, 2025
event.summary: On February 17, 2025, an Expro Americas Inc. employee (AP) was lifted approximately 100 feet on the forward man rider to tighten a leaking hydraulic hose on the Coil Tubing Lift Frame (CTLF) aboard the Noble Globetrotter drillship. After completing the task and signaling for descent, the man rider began descending at an accelerated rate. The hoist operator attempted to control the descent by moving the control to hoist position and attempting to activate the emergency stop, but AP reached the rig floor before intervention could be effective. Investigation revealed that the Original Equipment Manufacturer's Programming and Control Logic (PCL) had been altered from "Default" to "Warning" settings, allowing system faults to be overridden and preventing brake engagement. Contributing factors included lack of preventive maintenance, environmental degradation of equipment, inadequate pre-use inspections, and administrative deficiencies in the Control of Work permit.
key_phrases: PCL settings changed from Default to Warning

hazards.name: Pressurized hydraulic system with leaking hose
Man rider lifting equipment at height
Electrical and mechanical control systems for winch hoist

threats.name: Loss of control logic in hoist system due to altered OEM programming
Brake system failure due to control logic override
Environmental degradation of drive motor and encoder
Inadequate visibility of personnel during man riding operation
Failure to activate emergency stop during descent

consequences.name: Personnel injury from uncontrolled descent and impact with rig floor
Requirement for medical evacuation and evaluation
Potential fatality from fall from height

controls.name: OEM Programming and Control Logic (PCL) with safety settings
controls.barrier_role: Primary control to prevent uncontrolled descent
controls.lod_basis: Automated system to prevent brake override and control descent rate

supporting_text: The original equipment manufacture's (OEM's) Programing and Control Logic (PCL) was altered at some point

The OEM programing changed from 'Default' to 'Warning' settings. This allowed three separate system faults: Slack line, Upper / Lower Travel Limit, and Encoder performance / speed resolution to be overridden
This also prevented the control logic from engaging either of the two load brakes, stopping the initiation of the lift cycle, or stopping the lifting / lowering operation once it was underway

controls.name: Load brake system on hoist
controls.barrier_role: Secondary control to stop descent in emergency
controls.lod_basis: Mechanical brake to arrest load movement

supporting_text: Because of this bypass, the brakes did not engage allowing AP to be lowered uncontrolled

controls.name: Emergency stop button on hoist control console
controls.lod_basis: Manual emergency intervention to stop hoist operation

supporting_text: The hoist operator stated he attempted to hit the emergency stop, but AP was on the floor before the button could be pressed
the emergency stop was not activated during the event

controls.name: Preventive maintenance schedule for hoist equipment
controls.barrier_role: Maintenance program to detect and prevent equipment degradation
controls.lod_basis: Scheduled maintenance to identify and correct equipment defects

supporting_text: Lack of preventive maintenance by the contractor and insufficient inspections performed by subcontracted third-party inspectors were found to be contributing factors
Although the contractor has a preventative maintenance schedule in place, the final report pointed out lack of maintenance as another factor
The environmental degradation of the man rider should have been observed during maintenance checks and pre-use inspections

controls.name: Pre-use inspection of man rider and hoist equipment
controls.barrier_role: Detection of equipment defects before operation
controls.lod_basis: Visual and functional inspection to identify degraded or failed components

supporting_text: Insufficient inspections performed by subcontracted third-party inspectors were found to be contributing factors

The lack of maintenance and lack of attention during pre-use inspections led to the failure of the hoist operators to identify problems associated with the safety and functionality of the man rider and associated equipment
the encoder housing was cracked with a faulty connecting cable and the drive motor was so corroded that it was only performing at 10% of its original rated output

controls.name: Control of Work (COW) permit with visual contact requirement
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to ensure operator maintains visual contact during man riding
controls.lod_basis: Work permit requirement for continuous visual monitoring

supporting_text: The COW permit included a control to ensure the winch operator maintained visual with the person man riding at all times and prevented blind lifts
Despite this, the winch control console was positioned at an elevated location that obstructed the operator's view of the CTLF

controls.name: Job Safety Analysis (JSA) for man riding work at height
controls.barrier_role: Administrative control to identify hazards and plan safe work procedures
controls.lod_basis: Pre-work hazard assessment and procedure planning

supporting_text: A Job Safety Analysis (JSA) was completed and reviewed by Noble's Tour Pusher, Noble's banksman, hoist operator, and the AP
Neither the winch operator nor AP were specified in the COW. Although the Tour Pusher attended the transition to work meeting, he was not listed in the COW

controls.name: Secondary fall protection (harness/lanyard)
controls.barrier_role: Personal protective equipment to arrest fall
controls.lod_basis: Last-resort protection against personnel fall from height

supporting_text: No secondary fall protection was worn by AP

controls.name: Supervision of man riding operations by Tour Pusher
controls.barrier_role: Supervisory oversight of high-risk lifting operations
controls.lod_basis: Direct supervision to ensure compliance with procedures and safe practices

supporting_text: The Tour Pusher should supervise man riding operations as per contractor policy but was not present during the event
Supervision- Inadequate supervision on maintenance checks and pre-use inspections

notes.rules: JSON output only. mentioned fields must be evidence-based. Use null for unknown values.
